evidence: Dependency direction is clean and consistent: api → (exploration, query, config, model); exploration → (query, model, config); query → model; config → model. No violations observed.
ExplorationService interface in exploration package fully decouples api.ExplorationHandlers from SemanticFieldExplorer implementation — api has zero direct dependency on the concrete class.
CollocateQueryPort narrow port is used by ConcordanceHandlers and CorpusQueryHandlers instead of QueryExecutor — explicit, documented boundary discipline.

viz/Edge.java and viz/RelationEdgeType.java are byte-for-byte identical copies of model/exploration/Edge.java and model/exploration/RelationEdgeType.java. No code imports viz.Edge or viz.RelationEdgeType — both viz types are dead duplicates.

viz/package-info.java itself states: 'Domain data carriers (e.g. model/exploration/Edge, model/exploration/RelationEdgeType) live in model/exploration/ so that non-visualization layers (e.g. api/) can import them without creating an api→viz dependency.' The viz package has its own copies anyway, contradicting its own documented boundary contract.
RadialPlot.java (the only production consumer of viz/) uses only RadialPlot.Item (an inner class) — it never references viz.Edge or viz.RelationEdgeType. The duplicates serve no runtime purpose.
api/ExploreResponseAssembler.java imports model.exploration.Edge and model.exploration.RelationEdgeType correctly — confirming the canonical types are the model ones.

strengths: Dependency direction is uniformly top-down (api → exploration/query → model/config) with no cycles or reverse dependencies observed across the entire codebase.
ExplorationService interface cleanly separates the api package from the exploration implementation, so the HTTP layer never touches SemanticFieldExplorer directly.

CollocateQueryPort narrow-port discipline: ConcordanceHandlers and CorpusQueryHandlers declare the narrowest interface they need rather than the full QueryExecutor, making their dependency surface explicit.

Handler decomposition in api is well-bounded: SketchHandlers, ExplorationHandlers, ConcordanceHandlers, CorpusQueryHandlers, and VisualizationHandlers each own one coherent concern with no visible cross-pollution.
viz/package-info.java explicitly documents the intended boundary (canonical types live in model/exploration) — the architecture intent is recorded, even if execution is incomplete.
cross_module_architecture.issue_character: A single, self-acknowledged boundary drift: viz.Edge and viz.RelationEdgeType are orphaned identical copies of model.exploration types that the viz package-info explicitly says belong in model/exploration; the drift is contained to one package but contradicts the documented contract.
cross_module_architecture.score_rationale: The layering, interface abstractions, and narrow-port discipline are all solid — this codebase is clearly above the 'significant drag' threshold. The orphaned duplicates in viz are the one concrete boundary violation: they are dead code, their own package-info refutes them, and they represent a documented architecture contract that was not fully carried through. This is a single module-scoped issue rather than a systemic pattern, so it pulls the score modestly below 'solid but uneven (80)' — landing around 73 where the architecture is largely trustworthy but has one unresolved residue that a developer maintaining viz would eventually hit.

issues.summary: viz.Edge and viz.RelationEdgeType are dead identical copies of model.exploration types that viz/package-info.java itself says belong in model/exploration
evidence: viz/Edge.java and model/exploration/Edge.java have identical record bodies (source, target, weight, type fields; identical Javadoc).
viz/RelationEdgeType.java and model/exploration/RelationEdgeType.java have identical enum constants (SEED_COLLOCATE, DISCOVERED_COLLOCATE, MODIFIER) and identical label/toString logic.

viz/package-info.java states explicitly: 'Domain data carriers (e.g. model/exploration/Edge, model/exploration/RelationEdgeType) live in model/exploration/ so that non-visualization layers (e.g. api/) can import them without creating an api→viz dependency.' The viz copies exist anyway.
grep across all production and test sources finds zero imports of viz.Edge or viz.RelationEdgeType — both types are unreferenced dead code.
issues.suggestion: Delete viz/Edge.java and viz/RelationEdgeType.java. All consumers (ExploreResponseAssembler, ExplorationHandlersTest, ExploreResponseAssemblerTest) already import from model.exploration — no import changes needed. This completes the refactoring that viz/package-info.java already documents as the intended end-state.

root_causes: Incremental extraction: the viz package predates the model/exploration split, and Edge/RelationEdgeType were moved to model/exploration without deleting the originals in viz. The package-info was updated to document the new intent but the old files were not removed.
likely_symptoms: A future developer adding a new field to model/exploration/Edge may accidentally miss the identical viz/Edge copy, causing silent divergence.
